entries.name: alpha
entries.means: A small tuning number — often a learning rate or a mixing weight.

entries.name: beta
entries.means: Another tuning number; also the weights in classical statistics, and momentum's decay factor.

entries.name: delta
entries.means: "The change in" — Δx is "how much x changed." Lowercase δ often marks a small error signal (backprop's per-neuron blame).

entries.name: epsilon
entries.means: A tiny amount: leftover noise, or a small constant added to avoid dividing by zero.

entries.name: eta
entries.means: The learning rate — how big each training nudge is.

entries.name: theta
entries.means: "All the model's settings, bundled into one name." When you see θ, think "the knobs."

entries.name: lambda
entries.means: A strength dial — classically, how hard regularization punishes dramatic settings.

entries.name: mu
entries.means: An average. μ with a subscript (μ_c) is the average of one group — a cluster center.

entries.name: sigma
entries.means: Two jobs, context decides: the standard deviation (spread around the average), OR the sigmoid squashing function σ(z).
aliases: little o with a hat handle

entries.name: sigma
entries.means: "Sum of": add up what follows, once per item in the range shown below and above it.

entries.name: pi
entries.means: 3.14159…, and in reinforcement learning, "a policy" — a strategy for choosing actions.

entries.name: phi
entries.means: Conventionally, a transformation applied to inputs — "the features, after processing."
aliases: circle with a stick through it

entries.name: equals
entries.means: The sentence's verb. Split the equation here: left = the thing being defined, right = the recipe.

entries.name: approximately equals
entries.means: Equal for practical purposes — often exact in the limit as some nudge shrinks to nothing.

entries.name: assignment arrow
entries.means: An update: replace the left side with the result on the right. The = of code, in math clothes.

entries.name: summation
entries.means: Add up what follows, once per value of the counter. A for-loop in a toga.

entries.name: product
entries.means: Same as ∑, but multiply instead of add.

entries.name: element of
entries.means: Membership: "x ∈ D" reads "x is one of the items in D."

entries.name: absolute value
entries.means: Drop the minus sign: how big, ignoring direction.

entries.name: norm
entries.means: The length of a vector (an arrow). ‖a − b‖ is the distance between two points.

entries.name: hat
entries.means: "Our estimate of x" — the hat marks a guess. ŷ is the model's prediction of the true y.

entries.name: subscript
entries.means: Item number i in a list. The subscript is a counter, nothing more.

entries.name: superscript / power
entries.means: Repeated multiplication: x·x. Squaring makes everything positive and punishes big values extra — why it's inside so many losses.

entries.name: square root
entries.means: Undo a squaring: √9 = 3. Often used to return to ordinary units after squaring gaps.

entries.name: partial derivative
entries.means: "How this changes as that changes," nudging one input while the others hold still. ∂L/∂w = the loss's sensitivity to one weight.

entries.name: nabla / gradient
entries.means: All the partial derivatives at once, bundled into one vector. Points uphill; training steps the other way.

entries.name: expected value
entries.means: The long-run average of something random, weighted by how likely each outcome is.

entries.name: argmin
entries.means: "The setting that makes the following smallest (largest)" — not the small value itself, but WHICH knob position achieves it. Training is one giant argmin of the loss.

entries.name: logarithm
entries.means: The undo of e-to-the-power. In losses: gentle near 1 (small penalty for near-certainty in the truth), brutal near 0 (huge penalty for confident wrongness).

entries.name: Euler's number
entries.means: ≈ 2.718, the constant nature keeps choosing. e⁻ᶻ shrinks fast as z grows — the engine inside sigmoid and softmax.

entries.name: the input
entries.means: What we predict from. Bold 𝐱 = the whole input vector (all features).

entries.name: the true answer
entries.means: The label: what actually happened. The model's guesses are graded against it.

entries.name: the prediction
entries.means: The model's guess at y. Hat = estimate.

entries.name: weight(s)
entries.means: How much an input matters. Bold = one weight per feature; 𝐰·𝐱 is the dot product at the heart of nearly every model.

entries.name: the bias
entries.means: The starting offset — the prediction when every input is zero. (Unrelated to dataset bias; the word is overworked.)

entries.name: the loss
entries.means: The model's grade: how wrong it is, as one number. Lower is better. Also seen as J (an older convention for the same idea).

entries.name: the count
entries.means: How many: data points, features, whatever's being counted. Dividing by n turns totals into averages.

entries.name: the sigmoid
entries.means: The S-shaped squasher: any score in, a 0-to-1 probability out.

entries.name: softmax
entries.means: Scores in, percentages out (positive, summing to 100%). Sigmoid's many-option sibling: attention weights, classifiers, next-token probabilities.

entries.name: query, key, value
entries.means: Attention's three roles: what I'm looking for, what each word advertises, what each word actually hands over.

entries.name: distance
entries.say: the distance between a and b
entries.means: How far apart two data points are — usually the straight-line (Euclidean) distance.

entries.name: activation function
entries.means: The bend after a neuron's weighted sum: ReLU, tanh, sigmoid. Without it, stacked layers collapse into one.
